metadata.title: Conservation Law: Information Flux Through TZP
metadata.creator: Daniel Alexander Trawin

master_row.title: Conservation Law: Information Flux Through TZP
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as an integral expression relating Γ, μ, ν, I, T.
master_row.dictionary: The information flux (I) through the TZP satisfies (dIdt=0), where (I=_ T^_,d_) and (T^) is the stress–energy tensor of the information field and (^) the timelike Killing vector.
master_row.encyclopedia: Technical interpretation remains tied to the local registry and equation witness. Interpretive role: This law is the registry's explicit anti-information-loss principle at the zero point. It lets later entropy, quantum, and vacuum entries assume that information can be rearranged but not destroyed by the anchor.
